speaker: MATT
raw_text: You begin humming your favorite eye-pulling song under your breath and with a horrible sucking sound, you pull the eye from her socket, leaving this trail of dark black and red viscera that dangles from it loosely. As you pull it free, whatever attached the eye to it seems to burn off and fall, leaving just the circular socket. I also need you to roll a wisdom saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Should we take it out?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes. It could be Vecna, watching us.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Does it look reptilian?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can you put it in a bag or something? Cover it up?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, can I take a closer look at it and see if I can, I don't know, identify it somehow?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you have an Identify spell?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, but can I just see?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an arcana check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure. Two! Plus five.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you get down close to glance at the rather disturbingly bloodied face of Delilah Briarwood, the eye shifts back towards you. The closer you get, you watch as the thinning slit of the pupil seems to widen and focus on you. It's an unsettling presence. As you try and concentrate to see if there's any immediate signs of whether it be sigils or arcane marks that would mark it to be more of a magically enchanted object than an actual organic eye, nothing comes to mind other than the fact that it seems to be very intent on studying you back.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I try to remove it from the ocular cavity?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Using your fingers?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. My tiny, ocular hands.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Go ahead and make an athletics check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, you (slurping).

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I can inspire myself, I suppose.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you want to. Up to you. How many more bardic inspirations do you have after the battle?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Two, right? I didn't Cutting Words anyone. Those count as inspiration, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Your fingers are now knuckle-deep in Delilah Briarwood's socket.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll inspire myself just to give it a serious try. That's plus ten, so that's a 19 plus, I guess, three for Jack of All Trades?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you're not trained in it, then yeah.
